brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +47.9% since Feb 27 — Brent ~$105 (Apr 1 intraday); down 5% on Trump ceasefire claims and peace hopes; Mar 31 close $110.69; +55% in March — record since 1988
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 31 close: $110.69/bbl. Apr 1: Brent dipped to $104.86-105.27 on Trump ceasefire claims and surging markets. March +55% — largest monthly gain since inception in 1988. Goldman: $110 avg through March-April, settling to $71 Q4 if crisis resolves. Sparta Commodities analyst: 'ceasefire does not equal supply restoration — physical relief is weeks-to-months away.' Apr 6 energy strike deadline 5 days away. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / Sparta Commodities.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +51.9% since Feb 27 — WTI ~$101 (Apr 1 intraday); Mar 31 close $101.39; +~48% in March — largest since 2020; testing below $100 on peace hopes
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Mar 31 close: $101.39/bbl. Apr 1: WTI testing $98.79-102.92 range on Trump ceasefire rhetoric. WTI +~48% in March — largest monthly surge since 2020. Gas at $4.064/gal nationally (Apr 1) — largest monthly jump on record ($1.08). Diesel $5.49/gal. California ~$5.89/gal. Apr 6 energy strike deadline 5 days away. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.08 / +36.4% since Feb 27 — $4.064 nationally (Apr 1) — largest monthly jump on record — CA ~$5.89 — diesel $5.49 — first >$4 since Aug 2022
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 1: $4.064/gal nationally — first time above $4 since August 2022. Monthly jump of $1.08 exceeds steepest climbs after Hurricane Katrina (2005) and Russia-Ukraine invasion (2022). Mid-grade $4.578. Premium $4.943. Diesel $5.490. California ~$5.89/gal. Year ago: $3.201. Source: AAA / EIA.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -350 pts / -5.1% — S&P 6,528.52 (Mar 31 close); +2.91% best day since May on peace hopes; Apr 1 +0.6-0.9%; VIX 25.25 → ~24.4; still negative Q1
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Mar 31 close: 6,528.52 (+2.91% — best single-day gain since May, driven by Trump '2-3 weeks' comments). Apr 1 intraday: ~6,573-6,590 (+0.6-0.9%). Dow 46,341.51 (+2.49%). Nasdaq 21,590.63 (+3.83%). VIX collapsed from 30.61 to 25.25 (−17.5%), Apr 1 ~24.4. 10Y Treasury 4.34% → 4.31%. Asian markets surged: Kospi +8.4%, Nikkei +5.2%. S&P still fell >5% in March and posted negative Q1 2026. Moody's recession 49%. Goldman recession odds 25-30%. RTX ~$192.90 (+3.1%). Gold $4,746-4,766 (+$87). Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / Yahoo Finance.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — 200 dynamic strikes in 24 hrs — 82nd Airborne HQ + BCT deploying — Kharg Island seizure under consideration — $50B supplemental being prepared — Apr 6 deadline 5 days
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 33: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. Hegseth disclosed 200 'dynamic strikes' in 24 hrs — targets received mid-flight from real-time intelligence. 11,000+ US targets struck in first 30 days. IDF: 800+ attack sorties, ~16,000 munitions dropped. 82nd Airborne HQ + BCT (~3,000 paratroopers) confirmed deploying. Pentagon preparing plans for 'weeks of ground operations in Iran' — Kharg Island seizure under serious consideration. $50B supplemental being prepared. $200B Hegseth request. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / WaPo.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (33 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$39.3B — IranWarCost.com tracker: ~$36B incrementing ~$200M/day — Al Jazeera: $31.35-33.77B including Israel support
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 33: ~$39.3B. IranWarCost.com tracker: ~$36B incrementing ~$200M/day. Al Jazeera: $31.35-33.77B including Israel support operations. First six days alone cost $11.3B per Pentagon briefings to Congress. $50B supplemental being prepared; $200B Hegseth request. IEA: 32 member states released 400M barrels from strategic reserves. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / IranWarCost.com / Al Jazeera.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 11,000+ US targets in 30 days — 200 dynamic strikes in 24 hrs — IDF: 800+ sorties, ~16,000 munitions — former US Embassy compound Tehran struck — Tofigh Daru pharma factory — Qeshm desalination
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM (Mar 31 briefing): 11,000+ US targets in first 30 days. 200 'dynamic strikes' in last 24 hrs — targets assigned mid-flight. IDF: 800+ attack sorties, ~16,000 munitions dropped on Iran since Feb 28. Day 33 strikes: former US Embassy compound Tehran (internally detonating penetrator), Tofigh Daru pharmaceutical factory Tehran (10 nurses killed), Kerman/Sirjan, Ahvaz/Mahshahr, Shahid Haqqani passenger pier Bandar Abbas, Qeshm Island desalination plant. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~32,000+ through Day 33. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / Anadolu.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 15 US KIA — 6 Kuwait (Mar 1) + 6 KC-135 crash (Mar 12) + 1 died of wounds (Mar 9) + 1 non-hostile (Mar 4) + 1 health-related (Mar 6) — 348 WIA (315 RTD, 6 seriously)
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 15 US KIA confirmed: 6 soldiers from 103rd ESC killed in Iranian drone strike near Camp Arifjan, Kuwait (Mar 1); 6 airmen killed when KC-135 tanker crashed in western Iraq (Mar 12); 1 died of wounds (Mar 9); 1 non-hostile death (Mar 4); 1 health-related (Mar 6). 348 wounded (315 returned to duty ~91%; 6 remain seriously wounded). Day 32-33 jump from 290 to 348 WIA reflects Prince Sultan AB strike (6 BMs + 29 drones, 15+ US troops wounded including 5 seriously). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 348 WIA (315 RTD ~91%, 6 seriously) — 15 KIA — 82nd Airborne HQ + BCT deploying — Tripoli ARG operational — Boxer en route — Bush CSG departed Norfolk
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. 348 WIA per Pentagon Mar 31 briefing (315 returned to duty ~91%; 6 remain seriously wounded). Up from 290 over 24 hours — reflects Prince Sultan AB strike (6 BMs + 29 drones). 82nd Airborne HQ + BCT (~3,000 paratroopers) deploying. Tripoli ARG/31st MEU (~3,500) operational in CENTCOM. Boxer ARG/11th MEU en route from San Diego. USS George H.W. Bush CSG departed Norfolk Mar 31. Pentagon preparing 'weeks of ground operations.' Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / USNI Fleet Tracker.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 1,937 killed, 24,800+ wounded — Hengaw: 6,530 (640 civilians) — HRANA: 3,114 (1,354 civilians) — Iran Int'l: 4,700+ security forces — 289 health facilities damaged — 3.2M displaced
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources. Iranian Health Ministry (deputy minister): 1,937 killed, 24,800+ wounded. Hengaw Organization (25th day): 6,530 killed including 640 civilians. HRANA (by Mar 17): 3,114 deaths including 1,354 civilians. Iran International (Mar 31): 4,700+ security forces killed. OCHA (Mar 25): 289 health facilities damaged, 50+ primary care centers + 5 hospitals closed, 87,000+ civilian units damaged. WHO: 53 healthcare worker deaths, 117 injuries in 77 attacks. 3.2 million displaced. True toll likely between official figures and NGO estimates. Source: Iran Health Ministry / Hengaw / HRANA / Iran International / OCHA / WHO.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,200+ killed (107+ children) — 3,000+ wounded — over 1M displaced (~20% of pop.) — Hezbollah Southern Front cmdr Hashem killed in Beirut naval strike — 472 schools used as shelters
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and ground invasion since March 2. 1,200+ killed per Lebanese MoH (107+ children). 3,000+ wounded. Over 1 million displaced (~20% of population). Day 33: Israel killed Hajj Yusuf Ismail Hashem, Hezbollah Southern Front commander, in naval strike on Beirut — most senior Hezbollah figure eliminated ('40+ years experience'). Second senior terrorist killed in separate Beirut strike. Jnah neighborhood strike: 7 killed, 26 wounded. Hezbollah rocket hit Kiryat Shmona home. DM Katz announced permanent 'security zone'; border village homes to be demolished. 472 schools used as shelters. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNHCR / WHO.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.4M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M — Lebanon 1.2M+ (quarter of population) — 70,000+ Afghan refugees expelled — global humanitarian aid disrupted
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. 4.4M+ total displaced. Iran: ~3.2 million internally displaced. Lebanon: over 1.2 million (~25% of population). 70,000+ Afghan refugees expelled from Iran. Global humanitarian aid disrupted: WFP food for Afghan children stuck, WHO medicine for Gaza held up, IRC supplies for Sudan stranded in Dubai. Dubai's International Humanitarian City effectively crippled. Shipping containers face $3,000 emergency surcharge. OCHA activated contingency plans across 7 countries. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / WaPo / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — ~400 vessels stranded in Gulf of Oman — ~20,000 seafarers waiting — insurance 5%+ of vessel value — Spain closes airspace to US planes
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. ~400 vessels stranded in Gulf of Oman with ~20,000 seafarers waiting. Insurance rates surged from 0.25-0.5% to 5%+ of vessel value per transit. Iran 'tollbooth' system charging ~$2M per transit in Chinese yuan. Houthi Bab al-Mandeb closure 'among our options' — dual chokepoint scenario. Spain closed airspace to US planes involved in Iran attacks (Mar 30). Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's List / S&P Global.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 228+ children (Iran) + Lebanon 107+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — Bnei Brak cluster bomb critically wounds 10-12yr girl and 13yr boy — 600+ schools attacked — UNHRC investigating
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 228+ children killed in Iran. Lebanon: 107+ children. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Day 33: IRGC cluster bomb warhead struck Bnei Brak — 10-12yr girl critically injured by glass shrapnel in her bed (Sheba Medical: 'teams fighting for her life'), 13yr boy seriously wounded trying to wake deaf mother. Minab school (Feb 28): 100+ schoolchildren aged 7-12 killed. 600+ educational centers attacked in Iran per Red Crescent. 600 schools damaged. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / ICRC / Sheba Medical Center.
